English Example: If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.
Spanish Meaning: Un muelle es una estructura que se extiende hacia un cuerpo de agua.
Spanish Example: Si quieres pescar peces más grandes, ve al otro extremo del muelle.
Spanish Meaning (English): A pier is a structure that extends into a body of water.
Spanish Example (English): If you want to catch bigger fish, then go to the far end of the pier.

English Meaning: A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
English Example: We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.
Spanish Meaning: Un atajo es una ruta más corta que la ruta principal.
Spanish Example: Llegamos temprano a la casa porque tomamos un atajo por el bosque.
Spanish Meaning (English): A shortcut is a route that is shorter than the main route.
Spanish Example (English): We got to the house early because we took a shortcut through the forest.
Spanish Forms: singular: el atajo; plural: los atajos

English Meaning: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
English Example: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.
Spanish Meaning: Un arancel es un impuesto o tasa que se paga sobre determinadas importaciones o exportaciones.
Spanish Example: Nuestro arancel sobre la lana importada encarece la lana extranjera.
Spanish Meaning (English): A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports.
Spanish Example (English): Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive.
Spanish Forms: singular: el arancel; plural: los aranceles

English Meaning: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.
English Example: The thermometer outside the window indicated that it was a hot day today.
Spanish Meaning: Un termómetro es un dispositivo que mide la temperatura.
Spanish Example: El termómetro fuera de la ventana indicó que hoy era un día caluroso.
Spanish Meaning (English): A thermometer is a device that measures temperature.

English Meaning: To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.
English Example: She tilted her glass and almost spilled some of the wine inside.
Spanish Meaning: Inclinar algo significa moverlo para que no quede recto o vertical.
Spanish Example: Inclinó su copa y casi derramó parte del vino que contenía.
Spanish Meaning (English): To tilt something means to tip it into a sloping position.

English Meaning: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
English Example: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.
Spanish Meaning: Si una afirmación es al revés, entonces sus dos ideas principales se intercambian entre sí.
Spanish Example: Los estudiantes aprenden de sus profesores y viceversa.
Spanish Meaning (English): If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another.
Spanish Example (English): Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa.

English Meaning: The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
English Example: The police looked for the lost dog, but its whereabouts were still unknown.
Spanish Meaning: El paradero de alguien o algo es el lugar donde se encuentra.
Spanish Example: La policía buscó al perro perdido, pero aún se desconocía su paradero.
Spanish Meaning (English): The whereabouts of someone or something is the place where they are.
